mnemonics: C.L.O.U.D. - Consider Latency, Overhead, Usage patterns, and Dependencies for capacity planning
keyPoints: Cloud capacity is elastic and on-demand, allowing for rapid scaling up or down
Pay-per-use model shifts capital expenditure (CapEx) to operational expenditure (OpEx)
Auto-scaling and load balancing are key tools for managing cloud capacity efficiently
commonMistakes: Assuming infinite cloud capacity without monitoring for throttling or service limits
Over-provisioning resources 'just to be safe,' leading to unnecessary costs
Failing to plan for data transfer costs and latency between cloud regions or availability zones
